121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166	
167	
168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
201	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
202	        # at least one
203	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
204	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
205	
206	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
207	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
208	    return logits
209	
210	
211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits
234

236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)

Perfect! Now I have all the information. Let me compile the complete findings for you:

## Report: vLLM V1 Sampling Metadata and Top-K/Top-P Implementation

Here's the exact content and line numbers for all requested files:

### 1. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478537408-48zqul/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

**Full content with line numbers (1-297)** - Complete file provided above.

Key components:
- **TopKTopPSampler class** (lines 22-118): Main module with platform-specific implementations
- **forward_native()** (lines 69-83): PyTorch-native top-k/top-p sampling
- **forward_cuda()** (lines 85-107): FlashInfer-optimized implementation
- **forward_tpu()** (lines 109-118): TPU-optimized implementation
- **apply_top_k_top_p()** (lines 168-208): Main masking algorithm
- **apply_top_k_top_p_tpu()** (lines 121-165): TPU-specific masking
- **apply_top_k_only()** (lines 211-233): Efficient top-k without sorting entire vocab
- **random_sample()** (lines 236-257): Rejection sampling using exponential distribution
- **flashinfer_sample()** (lines 260-296): FlashInfer-based sampling

### 2. V1's SamplingMetadata Construction with `top_p` and `top_k` Population

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478537408-48zqul/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py`

**Function:** `_make_sampling_metadata()` (lines 600-659)

```python
def _make_sampling_metadata(self) -> SamplingMetadata:
    num_reqs = self.num_reqs
    if not self.all_greedy:
        temperature = copy_slice(self.temperature_cpu_tensor,
                                 self.temperature, num_reqs)
    else:
        temperature = None
    if not self.no_top_p:
        copy_slice(self.top_p_cpu_tensor, self.top_p, num_reqs)
    if not self.no_top_k:
        copy_slice(self.top_k_cpu_tensor, self.top_k, num_reqs)

    if not self.no_penalties:
        # Since syncing these tensors is expensive only copy them
        # if necessary i.e. if there are requests which require
        # penalties to be applied during sampling.
        copy_slice(self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor,
                   self.frequency_penalties, num_reqs)
        copy_slice(self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor,
                   self.presence_penalties, num_reqs)
        copy_slice(self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor,
                   self.repetition_penalties, num_reqs)

    needs_prompt_token_ids = (not self.no_penalties or
                              (self.num_reqs > 0
                               and self.logits_processing_needs_token_ids))
    if needs_prompt_token_ids:
        # The prompt tokens are used only for applying penalties or
        # step pooling during the sampling/pooling process.
        # Hence copy these tensors only when there are requests which
        # need penalties/step_pooler to be applied.
        prompt_token_ids = self._make_prompt_token_ids_tensor()
    else:
        prompt_token_ids = None

    allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
    if not self.no_allowed_token_ids:
        assert self.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None
        copy_slice(self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor,
                   self.allowed_token_ids_mask, num_reqs)
        allowed_token_ids_mask = self.allowed_token_ids_mask[:num_reqs]

    return SamplingMetadata(
        temperature=temperature,
        all_greedy=self.all_greedy,
        all_random=self.all_random,
        top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs],
        top_k=None if self.no_top_k else self.top_k[:num_reqs],
        generators=self.generators,
        max_num_logprobs=self.max_num_logprobs,
        prompt_token_ids=prompt_token_ids,
        frequency_penalties=self.frequency_penalties[:num_reqs],
        presence_penalties=self.presence_penalties[:num_reqs],
        repetition_penalties=self.repetition_penalties[:num_reqs],
        output_token_ids=cast(list[list[int]], self.req_output_token_ids),
        no_penalties=self.no_penalties,
        allowed_token_ids_mask=allowed_token_ids_mask,
        bad_words_token_ids=self.bad_words_token_ids,
        logitsprocs=self.logitsprocs,
    )
```

**Top-p and top-k population details:**
- Lines 646-647: Tensors are sliced to `num_reqs` and conditionally assigned based on `no_top_p` and `no_top_k` flags
- The source data comes from: `self.top_p_cpu` and `self.top_k_cpu` (numpy arrays, lines 140, 150)
- These are populated in the `add_request()` method (lines 312-320)

### 3. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478537408-48zqul/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` lines 386-597

**Lines 386-495: `from_sampling_metadata()` classmethod**

```python
    @classmethod
    def from_sampling_metadata(
        cls,
        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
        output_tokens: list[array] = []
        top_ks: list[int] = []
        temperatures: list[float] = []
        top_ps: list[float] = []
        min_ps: list[float] = []
        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
        do_penalties = False
        do_top_p_top_k = False
        do_min_p = False

        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
            min_p = sampling_params.min_p

            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
                temperature = 1.0
            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
                do_top_p_top_k = True
            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
                do_min_p = True
            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
                do_penalties = True

            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
                # their logprobs
                query_len = seq_group.query_len
                assert query_len is not None
                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
```

```python
    def _init_sampling_tensors(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ):
        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
        decodes of the same sequences.

        _, vocab_size = logits.shape

        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
        # have pinned memory.
        self._sampling_tensors = None

         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)

        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
        self._do_min_p = do_min_p
```

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478537408-48zqul/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py`

**Function:** `_make_sampling_metadata()` (lines 600-659)

```python
def _make_sampling_metadata(self) -> SamplingMetadata:
    num_reqs = self.num_reqs
    if not self.all_greedy:
        temperature = copy_slice(self.temperature_cpu_tensor,
                                 self.temperature, num_reqs)
    else:
        temperature = None
    if not self.no_top_p:
        copy_slice(self.top_p_cpu_tensor, self.top_p, num_reqs)
    if not self.no_top_k:
        copy_slice(self.top_k_cpu_tensor, self.top_k, num_reqs)

    if not self.no_penalties:
        # Since syncing these tensors is expensive only copy them
        # if necessary i.e. if there are requests which require
        # penalties to be applied during sampling.
        copy_slice(self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor,
                   self.frequency_penalties, num_reqs)
        copy_slice(self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor,
                   self.presence_penalties, num_reqs)
        copy_slice(self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor,
                   self.repetition_penalties, num_reqs)

    needs_prompt_token_ids = (not self.no_penalties or
                              (self.num_reqs > 0
                               and self.logits_processing_needs_token_ids))
    if needs_prompt_token_ids:
        # The prompt tokens are used only for applying penalties or
        # step pooling during the sampling/pooling process.
        # Hence copy these tensors only when there are requests which
        # need penalties/step_pooler to be applied.
        prompt_token_ids = self._make_prompt_token_ids_tensor()
    else:
        prompt_token_ids = None

    allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
    if not self.no_allowed_token_ids:
        assert self.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None
        copy_slice(self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor,
                   self.allowed_token_ids_mask, num_reqs)
        allowed_token_ids_mask = self.allowed_token_ids_mask[:num_reqs]

    return SamplingMetadata(
        temperature=temperature,
        all_greedy=self.all_greedy,
        all_random=self.all_random,
        top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs],
        top_k=None if self.no_top_k else self.top_k[:num_reqs],
        generators=self.generators,
        max_num_logprobs=self.max_num_logprobs,
        prompt_token_ids=prompt_token_ids,
        frequency_penalties=self.frequency_penalties[:num_reqs],
        presence_penalties=self.presence_penalties[:num_reqs],
        repetition_penalties=self.repetition_penalties[:num_reqs],
        output_token_ids=cast(list[list[int]], self.req_output_token_ids),
        no_penalties=self.no_penalties,
        allowed_token_ids_mask=allowed_token_ids_mask,
        bad_words_token_ids=self.bad_words_token_ids,
        logitsprocs=self.logitsprocs,
    )
```

**Top-p and top-k population details:**
- Lines 646-647: Tensors are sliced to `num_reqs` and conditionally assigned based on `no_top_p` and `no_top_k` flags
- The source data comes from: `self.top_p_cpu` and `self.top_k_cpu` (numpy arrays, lines 140, 150)
- These are populated in the `add_request()` method (lines 312-320)

```python
    @classmethod
    def from_sampling_metadata(
        cls,
        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
        output_tokens: list[array] = []
        top_ks: list[int] = []
        temperatures: list[float] = []
        top_ps: list[float] = []
        min_ps: list[float] = []
        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
        do_penalties = False
        do_top_p_top_k = False
        do_min_p = False

        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
            min_p = sampling_params.min_p

            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
                temperature = 1.0
            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
                do_top_p_top_k = True
            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
                do_min_p = True
            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
                do_penalties = True

            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
                # their logprobs
                query_len = seq_group.query_len
                assert query_len is not None
                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len

            if seq_group.do_sample:
                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens

        if do_penalties:
            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
                if (seq_group.is_prompt
                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                    prompt_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                    output_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                if seq_group.do_sample:
                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)

        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
            temperatures,
            top_ps,
            top_ks,
            min_ps,
            presence_penalties,
            frequency_penalties,
            repetition_penalties,
            prompt_tokens,
            output_tokens,
            vocab_size,
            device,
            dtype,
        )
        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)
```

266	    def add_request(

269	    ) -> int:
270	        req_index = self._register_add_request(request)
271	
272	        req_id = request.req_id

275	            self.req_output_token_ids.append(request.output_token_ids)
276	        else:
277	            self._req_ids[req_index] = req_id
278	            self.req_output_token_ids[req_index] = request.output_token_ids
279	
280	        self.req_id_to_index[req_id] = req_index

296	
297	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[req_index] = request.num_computed_tokens

305	                # Avoid later division by zero.
306	                self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = -1.0
307	                self.greedy_reqs.add(req_id)
308	            else:
309	                self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.temperature
310	                self.random_reqs.add(req_id)
311	
312	            self.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p
313	            if sampling_params.top_p < 1:
314	                self.top_p_reqs.add(req_id)
315	            top_k = sampling_params.top_k
316	            if 0 < top_k < self.vocab_size:
317	                self.top_k_reqs.add(req_id)
318	            else:
319	                top_k = self.vocab_size
320	            self.top_k_cpu[req_index] = top_k
321	            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
322	                req_index] = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
323	            if sampling_params.frequency_penalty != 0.0:
324	                self.frequency_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
325	            self.presence_penalties_cpu[
326	                req_index] = sampling_params.presence_penalty
327	            if sampling_params.presence_penalty != 0.0:
328	                self.presence_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
329	            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[
330	                req_index] = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
331	            if sampling_params.repetition_penalty != 1.0:
332	                self.repetition_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
333	
334	            # NOTE(woosuk): self.generators should not include the requests that

336	            if request.generator is not None:

338	
339	            if sampling_params.logprobs is not None:

341	            if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
342	                self.num_prompt_logprobs[
343	                    req_id] = sampling_params.prompt_logprobs
344	
345	            if sampling_params.allowed_token_ids:

367	                    req_index] = sampling_params.bad_words_token_ids
368	        else:
369	            assert request.pooling_params is not None

371	
372	        # Add request lora ID
373	        if request.lora_request:
374	            lora_id = request.lora_request.lora_int_id
375	            if lora_id not in self.lora_id_to_request_ids:

381	        else:
382	            # No LoRA
383	            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = 0
384	
385	        return req_index

387	    def remove_request(self, req_id: str) -> Optional[int]:
388	        """This method must always be followed by a call to condense().
389	        
390	        Args:
391	          req_id: request to remove
392	
393	        Returns:
394	          Removed request index, or `None` if `req_id` not recognized
395	        """
396	
397	        req_index = self.req_id_to_index.pop(req_id, None)
398	        if req_index is None:
399	            return None
400	        self.batch_update_builder.removed_append(req_index)
401	        self._req_ids[req_index] = None
402	        self.req_output_token_ids[req_index] = None
403	
404	        self.greedy_reqs.discard(req_id)
405	        self.random_reqs.discard(req_id)
406	        self.top_p_reqs.discard(req_id)
407	        self.top_k_reqs.discard(req_id)
408	        self.spec_decode_unsupported_reqs.discard(req_id)
409	        self.frequency_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
410	        self.presence_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
411	        self.repetition_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
412	        self.generators.pop(req_index, None)
413	        self.num_logprobs.pop(req_id, None)
414	        self.num_prompt_logprobs.pop(req_id, None)
415	        self.in_progress_prompt_logprobs_cpu.pop(req_id, None)
416	
417	        # LoRA
418	        lora_id = self.request_lora_mapping[req_index]
419	        if lora_id != 0:
420	            self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id].discard(req_id)
421	            if len(self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id]) == 0:
422	                self.lora_id_to_request_ids.pop(lora_id)
423	                self.lora_id_to_lora_request.pop(lora_id)
424	            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = 0
425	
426	        self.has_allowed_token_ids.discard(req_id)
427	        if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
428	            # False means we don't fill with -inf.
429	            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index].fill_(False)
430	        self.bad_words_token_ids.pop(req_index, None)
431	        self.pooling_params.pop(req_id, None)
432	        return req_index

600	    def _make_sampling_metadata(self) -> SamplingMetadata:
601	        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
602	        if not self.all_greedy:
603	            temperature = copy_slice(self.temperature_cpu_tensor,
604	                                     self.temperature, num_reqs)
605	        else:
606	            temperature = None
607	        if not self.no_top_p:
608	            copy_slice(self.top_p_cpu_tensor, self.top_p, num_reqs)
609	        if not self.no_top_k:
610	            copy_slice(self.top_k_cpu_tensor, self.top_k, num_reqs)
611	
612	        if not self.no_penalties:
613	            # Since syncing these tensors is expensive only copy them
614	            # if necessary i.e. if there are requests which require
615	            # penalties to be applied during sampling.
616	            copy_slice(self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor,
617	                       self.frequency_penalties, num_reqs)
618	            copy_slice(self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor,
619	                       self.presence_penalties, num_reqs)
620	            copy_slice(self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor,
621	                       self.repetition_penalties, num_reqs)
622	
623	        needs_prompt_token_ids = (not self.no_penalties or
624	                                  (self.num_reqs > 0
625	                                   and self.logits_processing_needs_token_ids))
626	        if needs_prompt_token_ids:
627	            # The prompt tokens are used only for applying penalties or
628	            # step pooling during the sampling/pooling process.
629	            # Hence copy these tensors only when there are requests which
630	            # need penalties/step_pooler to be applied.
631	            prompt_token_ids = self._make_prompt_token_ids_tensor()
632	        else:
633	            prompt_token_ids = None
634	
635	        allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
636	        if not self.no_allowed_token_ids:
637	            assert self.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None
638	            copy_slice(self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor,
639	                       self.allowed_token_ids_mask, num_reqs)
640	            allowed_token_ids_mask = self.allowed_token_ids_mask[:num_reqs]
641	
642	        return SamplingMetadata(
643	            temperature=temperature,
644	            all_greedy=self.all_greedy,
645	            all_random=self.all_random,
646	            top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs],
647	            top_k=None if self.no_top_k else self.top_k[:num_reqs],
648	            generators=self.generators,
649	            max_num_logprobs=self.max_num_logprobs,
650	            prompt_token_ids=prompt_token_ids,
651	            frequency_penalties=self.frequency_penalties[:num_reqs],
652	            presence_penalties=self.presence_penalties[:num_reqs],
653	            repetition_penalties=self.repetition_penalties[:num_reqs],
654	            output_token_ids=cast(list[list[int]], self.req_output_token_ids),
655	            no_penalties=self.no_penalties,
656	            allowed_token_ids_mask=allowed_token_ids_mask,
657	            bad_words_token_ids=self.bad_words_token_ids,
658	            logitsprocs=self.logitsprocs,
659	        )

1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
3	import dataclasses
4	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
5	from collections.abc import Iterator, Sequence
6	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
7	from enum import Enum
8	from itertools import chain
9	from typing import Optional, Union
10	
11	import torch
12	from torch._prims_common import DeviceLikeType
13	
14	from vllm import PoolingParams, SamplingParams
15	from vllm.logger import init_logger
16	
17	logger = init_logger(__name__)
18	
19	
20	class MoveDirectionality(Enum):
21	    # One-way i1->i2 req move within batch
22	    UNIDIRECTIONAL = 0
23	    # Two-way i1<->i2 req swap within batch
24	    SWAP = 1
25	
26	
27	# (index, params, output_tok_ids) tuples for new
28	# requests added to the batch.
29	AddedRequest = tuple[int, Union[SamplingParams, PoolingParams], list[int]]
30	# (index 1, index 2, directionality) tuples representing
31	# one-way moves or two-way swaps of requests in batch
32	MovedRequest = tuple[int, int, MoveDirectionality]
33	# Batch indices of any removed requests.
34	RemovedRequest = int
35	
36	
37	@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
38	class BatchUpdate:
39	    """Persistent batch state change info for logitsprocs"""
40	    batch_size: int  # Current num reqs in batch
41	
42	    # Metadata for requests added to, removed from, and moved
43	    # within the persistent batch.
44	    #
45	    # Note: each added request is represented as
46	    # (index, params, output_tok_ids)
47	    # Key assumption: output_tok_ids is a reference to the
48	    # request's running output tokens list; in this way
49	    # the logits processors always see the latest list of
50	    # generated tokens
51	    removed: Sequence[RemovedRequest]
52	    moved: Sequence[MovedRequest]
53	    added: Sequence[AddedRequest]
54	
55	
56	class BatchUpdateBuilder:
57	    """Helps track persistent batch state changes and build
58	    a batch update data structure for logitsprocs
59	    
60	    Assumptions:
61	    * All information about requests removed from persistent batch
62	      during a step is aggregated in self._removed through calls to
63	      self.removed_append() at the beginning of a step. This must happen
64	      before the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
65	      or self.peek_removed() are invoked in a given step
66	    * After the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
67	      or self.peek_removed() are read in a step, no new removals
68	      are registered using self.removed_append()
69	    * Elements of self._removed are never directly modified, added or
70	      removed (i.e. modification is only via self.removed_append() and
71	      self.pop_removed())
72	    
73	    Guarantees under above assumptions:
74	    * self.removed is always sorted in descending order
75	    * self.pop_removed() and self.peek_removed() both return
76	      the lowest removed request index in the current step
77	    """
78	
79	    _removed: list[RemovedRequest]
80	    _is_removed_sorted: bool
81	    moved: list[MovedRequest]
82	    added: list[AddedRequest]
83	
84	    def __init__(
85	        self,
86	        removed: Optional[list[RemovedRequest]] = None,
87	        moved: Optional[list[MovedRequest]] = None,
88	        added: Optional[list[AddedRequest]] = None,
89	    ) -> None:
90	        self._removed = removed or []
91	        self.moved = moved or []
92	        self.added = added or []
93	        self._is_removed_sorted = False
94	
95	    def _ensure_removed_sorted(self) -> None:
96	        """Sort removed request indices in
97	        descending order.
98	        
99	        Idempotent after first call in a
100	        given step, until reset.
101	        """
102	        if not self._is_removed_sorted:
103	            self._removed.sort(reverse=True)
104	            self._is_removed_sorted = True
105	
106	    @property
107	    def removed(self) -> list[RemovedRequest]:
108	        """Removed request indices sorted in
109	        descending order"""
110	        self._ensure_removed_sorted()
111	        return self._removed
112	
113	    def removed_append(self, index: int) -> None:
114	        """Register the removal of a request from
115	        the persistent batch.
116	
117	        Must not be called after the first time
118	        self.removed, self.pop_removed() or
119	        self.peek_removed() are invoked.
120	        
121	        Args:
122	          index: request index
123	        """
124	        if self._is_removed_sorted:
125	            raise RuntimeError("Cannot register new removed request after"
126	                               " self.removed has been read.")
127	        self._removed.append(index)
128	
129	    def has_removed(self) -> bool:
130	        return bool(self._removed)
131	
132	    def peek_removed(self) -> Optional[int]:
133	        """Return lowest removed request index"""
134	        if self.has_removed():
135	            self._ensure_removed_sorted()
136	            return self._removed[-1]
137	        return None
138	
139	    def pop_removed(self) -> Optional[int]:
140	        """Pop lowest removed request index"""
141	        if self.has_removed():
142	            self._ensure_removed_sorted()
143	            return self._removed.pop()
144	        return None
145	
146	    def get_and_reset(self, batch_size: int) -> Optional[BatchUpdate]:
147	        """Generate a logitsprocs batch update data structure
148	        and reset internal batch update builder state.
149	        
150	        Args:
151	          batch_size: current persistent batch size
152	
153	        Returns:
154	          Frozen logitsprocs batch update instance; `None` if no updates
155	        """
156	        # Reset removal-sorting logic
157	        self._is_removed_sorted = False
158	        if not any((self._removed, self.moved, self.added)):
159	            # No update; short-circuit
160	            return None
161	        # Build batch state update
162	        batch_update = BatchUpdate(
163	            batch_size=batch_size,
164	            removed=self._removed,
165	            moved=self.moved,
166	            added=self.added,
167	        )
168	        # Reset removed/moved/added update lists
169	        self._removed = []
170	        self.moved = []
171	        self.added = []
172	        return batch_update
173	
174	
175	class LogitsProcessor(ABC):
176	
177	    @abstractmethod
178	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
179	        raise NotImplementedError
180	
181	    @abstractmethod
182	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
183	        """True if logits processor has no impact on the
184	        argmax computation in greedy sampling.
185	        NOTE: may or may not have the same value for all
186	        instances of a given LogitsProcessor subclass,
187	        depending on subclass implementation.
188	        TODO(andy): won't be utilized until logits
189	        processors are user-extensible
190	        """
191	        raise NotImplementedError
192	
193	    @abstractmethod
194	    def update_state(
195	        self,
196	        batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate],
197	    ) -> None:
198	        """Called when there are new output tokens, prior
199	        to each forward pass.
200	
201	        Args:
202	            batch_update is non-None iff there have been
203	            changes to the batch makeup.
204	        """
205	        raise NotImplementedError
206	
207	
208	@dataclass
209	class LogitsProcessorManager:
210	    """Encapsulates initialized logitsproc objects."""
211	    argmax_invariant: list[LogitsProcessor] = field(
212	        default_factory=list)  # argmax-invariant logitsprocs
213	    non_argmax_invariant: list[LogitsProcessor] = field(
214	        default_factory=list)  # non-argmax-invariant logitsprocs
215	
216	    @property
217	    def all(self) -> Iterator[LogitsProcessor]:
218	        """Iterator over all logits processors."""
219	        return chain(self.argmax_invariant, self.non_argmax_invariant)
220	
221	
222	###### ----- Built-in LogitsProcessor impls below here
223	
224	
225	class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
226	
227	    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
228	                 device: DeviceLikeType):
229	        super().__init__()
230	        self.min_p_count: int = 0
231	
232	        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
233	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
234	                                            device="cpu",
235	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
236	        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
237	        # Pre-allocated device tensor
238	        self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
239	                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
240	                                                      device=device)
241	        # Current slice of the device tensor
242	        self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]
243	
244	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
245	        """Min-p never impacts greedy sampling"""
246	        return True
247	
248	    def get_min_p_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
249	        return float(self.min_p_cpu[index])
250	
251	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
252	        if not batch_update:
253	            return
254	
255	        needs_update = False
256	        # Process added requests.
257	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
258	            min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
259	            if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
260	                needs_update = True
261	                self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
262	            if min_p:
263	                self.min_p_count += 1
264	
265	        if self.min_p_count:
266	            # Process removed requests.
267	            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
268	            for index in batch_update.removed:
269	                if self.min_p_cpu[index]:
270	                    self.min_p_count -= 1
271	
272	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
273	            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
274	                change = (min_p_a :=
275	                          self.min_p_cpu[adx]) != (min_p_b :=
276	                                                   self.min_p_cpu[bdx])
277	                needs_update |= change
278	                if change:
279	                    self.min_p_cpu[bdx] = min_p_a
280	                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
281	                        self.min_p_cpu[adx] = min_p_b
282	
283	        # Update tensors if needed.
284	        size = batch_update.batch_size
285	        if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
286	            self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
287	            self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
288	            self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)
289	
290	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
291	        if not self.min_p_count:
292	            return logits
293	
294	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
295	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
296	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
297	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
298	                                       dim=-1,
299	                                       keepdim=True)
300	        # Adjust min_p
301	        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
302	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
303	        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
304	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
305	        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
306	        return logits
307	
308	
309	class LogitBiasLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
310	
311	    def __init__(self, pin_memory: bool, device: torch.device):
312	        super().__init__()
313	        self.biases: dict[int, dict[int, float]] = {}
314	        self.device = device
315	        self.pin_memory = pin_memory
316	
317	        self.bias_tensor: torch.Tensor = torch.tensor(())
318	        self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor([], torch.int32),
319	                             self._device_tensor([], torch.int32))
320	
321	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
322	        """Logit bias can rebalance token probabilities and change the
323	        outcome of argmax in greedy sampling."""
324	        return False
325	
326	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
327	        if not batch_update:
328	            return
329	
330	        # Process added requests.
331	        needs_update = bool(batch_update.added)
332	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
333	            if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) and (lb :=
334	                                                       params.logit_bias):
335	                self.biases[index] = lb
336	            else:
337	                self.biases.pop(index, None)
338	
339	        if self.biases:
340	            # Process removed requests.
341	            for index in batch_update.removed:
342	                if self.biases.pop(index, None):
343	                    needs_update = True
344	
345	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
346	            for a_index, b_index, direct in batch_update.moved:
347	                if direct == MoveDirectionality.UNIDIRECTIONAL:
348	                    if (a_entry := self.biases.pop(a_index, None)) is None:
349	                        if self.biases.pop(b_index, None) is not None:
350	                            needs_update = True
351	                    else:
352	                        self.biases[b_index] = a_entry
353	                        needs_update = True
354	                else:
355	                    a_entry = self.biases.pop(a_index, None)
356	                    if (b_entry := self.biases.pop(b_index, None)) is not None:
357	                        self.biases[a_index] = b_entry
358	                        needs_update = True
359	                    if a_entry is not None:
360	                        self.biases[b_index] = a_entry
361	                        needs_update = True
362	
363	        # Update tensors if needed.
364	        if needs_update:
365	            reqs, tok_ids, biases = [], [], []
366	            for req, lb in self.biases.items():
367	                reqs.extend([req] * len(lb))
368	                tok_ids.extend(lb.keys())
369	                biases.extend(lb.values())
370	
371	            self.bias_tensor = self._device_tensor(biases, torch.float32)
372	            self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor(reqs, torch.int32),
373	                                 self._device_tensor(tok_ids, torch.int32))
374	
375	    def _device_tensor(self, data: list, dtype: torch.dtype) -> torch.Tensor:
376	        return (torch.tensor(data,
377	                             device="cpu",
378	                             dtype=dtype,
379	                             pin_memory=self.pin_memory).to(device=self.device,
380	                                                            non_blocking=True))
381	
382	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
383	        if self.biases:
384	            logits[self.logits_slice] += self.bias_tensor
385	        return logits
386	
387	
388	class MinTokensLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
389	
390	    def __init__(self, pin_memory: bool, device: torch.device):
391	        # index -> (min_toks, output_token_ids, stop_token_ids)
392	        super().__init__()
393	        self.min_toks: dict[int, tuple[int, Sequence[int], set[int]]] = {}
394	        self.device = device
395	        self.pin_memory = pin_memory
396	
397	        # (req_idx_tensor,eos_tok_id_tensor)
398	        self.logits_slice: tuple[torch.Tensor,
399	                                 torch.Tensor] = (self._device_tensor(
400	                                     [], torch.int32),
401	                                                  self._device_tensor(
402	                                                      [], torch.int32))
403	
404	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
405	        """By censoring stop tokens, min-tokens can change the outcome
406	        of the argmax operation in greedy sampling."""
407	        return False
408	
409	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
410	        needs_update = False
411	
412	        if batch_update:
413	            # Process added requests.
414	            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.added)
415	            for index, params, output_tok_ids in batch_update.added:
416	                if (isinstance(params, SamplingParams)
417	                        and (min_tokens := params.min_tokens)
418	                        and len(output_tok_ids) < min_tokens):
419	                    # Replace request metadata at batch index
420	                    self.min_toks[index] = (min_tokens, output_tok_ids,
421	                                            params.all_stop_token_ids)
422	                else:
423	                    # Drop request metadata at batch index
424	                    self.min_toks.pop(index, None)
425	
426	            if self.min_toks:
427	                # Process removed requests.
428	                for index in batch_update.removed:
429	                    if self.min_toks.pop(index, None):
430	                        needs_update = True
431	
432	                # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and
433	                # swapped (a<->b)
434	                for a_index, b_index, direct in batch_update.moved:
435	                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.UNIDIRECTIONAL:
436	                        if (a_entry := self.min_toks.pop(a_index,
437	                                                         None)) is None:
438	                            if self.min_toks.pop(b_index, None) is not None:
439	                                needs_update = True
440	                        else:
441	                            self.min_toks[b_index] = a_entry
442	                            needs_update = True
443	                    else:
444	                        a_entry = self.min_toks.pop(a_index, None)
445	                        if (b_entry := self.min_toks.pop(b_index,
446	                                                         None)) is not None:
447	                            self.min_toks[a_index] = b_entry
448	                            needs_update = True
449	                        if a_entry is not None:
450	                            self.min_toks[b_index] = a_entry
451	                            needs_update = True
452	
453	        if self.min_toks:
454	            # Check for any requests that have attained their min tokens.
455	            to_remove = tuple(index for index, (min_toks, out_tok_ids,
456	                                                _) in self.min_toks.items()
457	                              if len(out_tok_ids) >= min_toks)
458	            if to_remove:
459	                needs_update = True
460	                for index in to_remove:
461	                    del self.min_toks[index]
462	
463	        # Update tensors if needed.
464	        if needs_update:
465	            reqs: list[int] = []
466	            tok_ids: list[int] = []
467	            for req, (_, _, stop_tok_ids) in self.min_toks.items():
468	                reqs.extend([req] * len(stop_tok_ids))
469	                tok_ids.extend(stop_tok_ids)
470	
471	            self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor(reqs, torch.int32),
472	                                 self._device_tensor(tok_ids, torch.int32))
473	
474	    def _device_tensor(self, data: list, dtype: torch.dtype) -> torch.Tensor:
475	        return (torch.tensor(data,
476	                             device="cpu",
477	                             dtype=dtype,
478	                             pin_memory=self.pin_memory).to(device=self.device,
479	                                                            non_blocking=True))
480	
481	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
482	        if self.min_toks:
483	            # Inhibit EOS token for requests which have not reached min length
484	            logits[self.logits_slice] = -float("inf")
485	        return logits
486	
487	
488	def init_builtin_logitsprocs(pin_memory_available: bool, max_num_reqs: int,
489	                             device: torch.device) -> LogitsProcessorManager:
490	    """Construct 'builtin' vLLM logitsprocs which the engine
491	    loads by default.
492	
493	    Args:
494	      pin_memory_available: pinned memory is available for use
495	                            for use by logitsproc
496	      max_num_reqs: ceiling on request count in persistent batch
497	      device: inference device
498	
499	    Returns:
500	      Data structure encapsulating loaded logitsprocs
501	    """
502	    min_tokens_logitproc = MinTokensLogitsProcessor(
503	        pin_memory=pin_memory_available, device=device)
504	    logit_bias_logitproc = LogitBiasLogitsProcessor(
505	        pin_memory=pin_memory_available, device=device)
506	    min_p_logitproc = MinPLogitsProcessor(
507	        pin_memory=pin_memory_available,
508	        device=device,
509	        # +1 for temporary swap space
510	        max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs + 1)
511	    return LogitsProcessorManager(
512	        non_argmax_invariant=[
513	            min_tokens_logitproc,
514	            logit_bias_logitproc,
515	        ],
516	        argmax_invariant=[min_p_logitproc],
517	    )
518

11	    temperature=-1.0,
12	    min_p=0.0,
13	    # strictly disabled for now
14	    top_k=0,
15	    top_p=1.0,
16	    # frequency_penalties=0.0,
17	    # presence_penalties=0.0,
18	    # repetition_penalties=0.0,
19	)
20	
21	
22	@dataclass
23	class TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata:
24	    # This class exposes a more xla-friendly interface than SamplingMetadata
25	    # on TPU, in particular all arguments should be traceable and no optionals
26	    # are allowed, to avoid graph recompilation on Nones.
27	    temperature: torch.Tensor = None
28	
29	    min_p: torch.Tensor = None
30	    top_k: torch.Tensor = None
31	    top_p: torch.Tensor = None
32	
33	    all_greedy: bool = True
34	
35	    # Whether logprobs are to be gathered in this batch of request. To balance
36	    # out compile time and runtime, a fixed `max_number_logprobs` value is used
37	    # when gathering logprobs, regardless of the values specified in the batch.
38	    logprobs: bool = False
39	
40	    # TODO No penalties for now
41	    no_penalties: bool = True
42	    prompt_token_ids = None
43	    frequency_penalties = None
44	    presence_penalties = None
45	    repetition_penalties = None
46	    # should use tensor
47	    output_token_ids: list[list[int]] = field(default_factory=lambda: list())
48	
49	    min_tokens = None  # impl is not vectorized
50	
51	    logit_bias: list[Optional[dict[int, float]]] = field(
52	        default_factory=lambda: list())
53	
54	    allowed_token_ids_mask = None
55	    bad_words_token_ids = None
56	
57	    # Generator not supported by xla
58	    _generators: dict[int,
59	                      torch.Generator] = field(default_factory=lambda: dict())
60	
61	    @property
62	    def generators(self) -> dict[int, torch.Generator]:
63	        # Generator not supported by torch/xla. This field must be immutable.
64	        return self._generators
65	
66	    @classmethod
67	    def from_input_batch(
68	        cls,
69	        input_batch: InputBatch,
70	        padded_num_reqs: int,
71	        xla_device: torch.device,
72	        generate_params_if_all_greedy: bool = False
73	    ) -> "TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata":
74	        """
75	        Copy sampling tensors slices from `input_batch` to on device tensors.
76	
77	        `InputBatch._make_sampling_metadata` causes recompilation on XLA as it 
78	        slices dynamic shapes on device tensors. This impl moves the dynamic 
79	        ops to CPU and produces tensors of fixed `padded_num_reqs` size.
80	
81	        Args:
82	            input_batch: The input batch containing sampling parameters.
83	            padded_num_reqs: The padded number of requests.
84	            xla_device: The XLA device.
85	            generate_params_if_all_greedy: If True, generate sampling parameters
86	                even if all requests are greedy. this is useful for cases where
87	                we want to pre-compile a graph with sampling parameters, even if
88	                they are not strictly needed for greedy decoding.
89	        """
90	        needs_logprobs = input_batch.max_num_logprobs>0 if \
91	            input_batch.max_num_logprobs else False
92	        # Early return to avoid unnecessary cpu to tpu copy
93	        if (input_batch.all_greedy is True
94	                and generate_params_if_all_greedy is False):
95	            return cls(all_greedy=True, logprobs=needs_logprobs)
96	
97	        num_reqs = input_batch.num_reqs
98	
99	        def fill_slice(cpu_tensor: torch.Tensor, fill_val) -> torch.Tensor:
100	            # Pad value is the default one.
101	            cpu_tensor[num_reqs:padded_num_reqs] = fill_val
102	
103	        fill_slice(input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor,
104	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["temperature"])
105	        fill_slice(input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor,
106	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["min_p"])
107	        fill_slice(input_batch.top_k_cpu_tensor,
108	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_k"])
109	        fill_slice(input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor,
110	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_p"])
111	
112	        # Slice persistent device tensors to a fixed pre-compiled padded shape.
113	        return cls(
114	            temperature=input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].
115	            to(xla_device),
116	            all_greedy=input_batch.all_greedy,
117	            # TODO enable more and avoid returning None values
118	            top_p=input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
119	                xla_device),
120	            top_k=input_batch.top_k_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
121	                xla_device),
122	            min_p=input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
123	                xla_device),
124	            logprobs=needs_logprobs)
125

3	"""Sampler layer implementing TPU supported operations."""
4	
5	import torch
6	import torch.nn as nn
7	
8	from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput
9	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler
10	from vllm.v1.sample.tpu.metadata import TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata
11	
12	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
13	
14	
15	class Sampler(nn.Module):
16	
17	    def __init__(self):
18	        super().__init__()
19	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
20	
21	    def forward(
22	        self,
23	        logits: torch.Tensor,
24	        sampling_metadata: TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata,
25	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
26	        # Use float32 for the logits.
27	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
28	        # Sample the next token.
29	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
30	
31	        # These are TPU tensors.
32	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
33	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
34	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
35	            # token per request.
36	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
37	            logprobs_tensors=None)
38	        return sampler_output
39	
40	    def apply_temperature(
41	        self,
42	        logits: torch.Tensor,
43	        temp: torch.Tensor,
44	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
45	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
46	
47	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
48	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
49	
50	    def sample(
51	        self,
52	        logits: torch.Tensor,
53	        sampling_metadata: TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata,
54	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
55	        greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
56	
57	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
58	
59	        # Apply temperature.
60	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
61	
62	        # Apply min_p.
63	        if sampling_metadata.min_p is not None:
64	            logits = self.apply_min_p(logits, sampling_metadata.min_p)
65	
66	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
67	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
68	            logits,
69	            sampling_metadata.generators,
70	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
71	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
72	        )
73	
74	        sampled = torch.where(sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
75	                              greedy_sampled, random_sampled)
76	        return sampled
77	
78	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
79	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
80	
81	    def gather_logprobs(
82	        self,
83	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
84	        num_logprobs: int,
85	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
86	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
87	        """
88	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
89	
90	        Args:
91	          logits: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
92	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
93	                        retain per token
94	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
95	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
96	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
97	                     with (num tokens) elements
98	
99	        Returns:
100	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
101	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
102	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
103	        """
104	        # Find the topK values.
105	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
106	                                                 num_logprobs,
107	                                                 dim=-1)
108	
109	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
110	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
111	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
112	
113	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
114	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
115	
116	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
117	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
118	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
119	
120	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
121	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
122	
123	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)
124	
125	    def apply_min_p(
126	        self,
127	        logits: torch.Tensor,
128	        min_p: torch.Tensor,
129	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
130	        """
131	        Filters logits using adaptive probability thresholding.
132	        """
133	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
134	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
135	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
136	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
137	                                       dim=-1,
138	                                       keepdim=True)
139	        # Reshape min_p for broadcasting
140	        adjusted_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
141	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
142	        valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_min_p
143	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing (xla friendly)
144	        logits.masked_fill_(~valid_token_mask, -float("inf"))
145	        return logits
146

3	# Datastructures defining a TPU input batch
4	
5	from typing import Optional, cast
6	
7	import numpy as np
8	import torch
9	
10	from vllm.lora.request import LoRARequest
11	from vllm.sampling_params import SamplingType
12	from vllm.utils import swap_dict_values
13	from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors
14	from vllm.v1.worker.block_table import MultiGroupBlockTable
15	from vllm.v1.worker.gpu_input_batch import CachedRequestState
16	
17	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
18	
19	
20	class InputBatch:
21	
22	    def __init__(
23	            self,
24	            max_num_reqs: int,
25	            max_model_len: int,
26	            max_num_batched_tokens: int,
27	            device: torch.device,
28	            pin_memory: bool,
29	            vocab_size: int,
30	            block_sizes: list[int],  # The block_size of each kv cache group
31	    ):
32	        self.max_num_reqs = max_num_reqs
33	        self.max_model_len = max_model_len
34	        self.max_num_batched_tokens = max_num_batched_tokens
35	        self.device = device
36	        self.pin_memory = pin_memory
37	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
38	
39	        self._req_ids: list[Optional[str]] = []
40	        self.req_id_to_index: dict[str, int] = {}
41	
42	        # TODO(woosuk): This buffer could be too large if max_model_len is big.
43	        # Find a way to reduce the CPU memory usage.
44	        # This buffer is not directly transferred to the GPU, so it does not
45	        # need to be pinned.
46	        self.token_ids_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
47	            (max_num_reqs, max_model_len),
48	            device="cpu",
49	            dtype=torch.int32,
50	            pin_memory=False,
51	        )
52	        self.token_ids_cpu = self.token_ids_cpu_tensor.numpy()
53	        self.num_tokens = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
54	        self.num_tokens_no_spec = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
55	        self.num_prompt_tokens = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
56	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
57	            (max_num_reqs, ),
58	            device="cpu",
59	            dtype=torch.int32,
60	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
61	        )
62	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu = \
63	            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu_tensor.numpy()
64	
65	        # Block table.
66	        self.block_table = MultiGroupBlockTable(
67	            max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs,
68	            max_model_len=max_model_len,
69	            max_num_batched_tokens=max_num_batched_tokens,
70	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
71	            device=device,
72	            block_sizes=block_sizes,
73	        )
74	
75	        # Sampling-related.
76	        self.temperature = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
77	                                       dtype=torch.float32,
78	                                       device=device)
79	        self.temperature_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
80	                                                  dtype=torch.float32,
81	                                                  device="cpu",
82	                                                  pin_memory=pin_memory)
83	        self.temperature_cpu = self.temperature_cpu_tensor.numpy()
84	        self.greedy_reqs: set[str] = set()
85	        self.random_reqs: set[str] = set()
86	
87	        self.top_p = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
88	                                 dtype=torch.float32,
89	                                 device=device)
90	        self.top_p_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
91	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
92	                                            device="cpu",
93	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
94	        self.top_p_cpu = self.top_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
95	        self.top_p_reqs: set[str] = set()
96	
97	        self.top_k = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
98	                                 dtype=torch.int32,
99	                                 device=device)
100	        self.top_k_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
101	                                            dtype=torch.int32,
102	                                            device="cpu",
103	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
104	        self.top_k_cpu = self.top_k_cpu_tensor.numpy()
105	        self.top_k_reqs: set[str] = set()
106	
107	        self.min_p = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
108	                                 dtype=torch.float32,
109	                                 device=device)
110	        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
111	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
112	                                            device="cpu",
113	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
114	        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
115	        self.min_p_reqs: set[str] = set()
116	
117	        # Frequency penalty related data structures
118	        self.frequency_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
119	                                               dtype=torch.float,
120	                                               device=device)
121	        self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
122	            (max_num_reqs, ),
123	            dtype=torch.float,
124	            device="cpu",
125	            pin_memory=pin_memory)
126	        self.frequency_penalties_cpu = \
127	            self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy()
128	        self.frequency_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()
129	
130	        # Presence penalty related data structures
131	        self.presence_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
132	                                              dtype=torch.float,
133	                                              device=device)
134	        self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
135	                                                         dtype=torch.float,
136	                                                         device="cpu",
137	                                                         pin_memory=pin_memory)
138	        self.presence_penalties_cpu = self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy(
139	        )
140	        self.presence_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()
141	
142	        # Repetition penalty related data structures
143	        self.repetition_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
144	                                                dtype=torch.float,
145	                                                device=device)
146	        self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
147	            (max_num_reqs, ),
148	            dtype=torch.float,
149	            device="cpu",
150	            pin_memory=pin_memory)
151	        self.repetition_penalties_cpu = \
152	            self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy()
153	        self.repetition_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()
154	
155	        # req_index -> (min_tokens, stop_token_ids)
156	        self.min_tokens: dict[int, tuple[int, set[int]]] = {}
157	
158	        # lora related
159	        self.request_lora_mapping = np.zeros((self.max_num_reqs, ),
160	                                             dtype=np.int32)
161	        self.lora_id_to_request_ids: dict[int, set[str]] = {}
162	        self.lora_id_to_lora_request: dict[int, LoRARequest] = {}
163	
164	        # req_index -> generator
165	        # NOTE(woosuk): The indices of the requests that do not have their own
166	        # generator should not be included in the dictionary.
167	        self.generators: dict[int, torch.Generator] = {}
168	
169	        self.num_logprobs: dict[str, int] = {}
170	        # NOTE(rob): num_prompt_logprobs only includes reqs
171	        # that are currently in the prefill phase.
172	        self.num_prompt_logprobs: dict[str, int] = {}
173	
174	        # To accumulate prompt logprobs tensor chunks across prefill steps.
175	        self.in_progress_prompt_logprobs_cpu: dict[str, LogprobsTensors] = {}
176	
177	        self.logit_bias: list[Optional[dict[int,
178	                                            float]]] = [None] * max_num_reqs
179	        self.has_allowed_token_ids: set[str] = set()
180	        # NOTE(lufang): In the mask tensor, if the corresponding token allowed,
181	        # the value is False. Since we use masked_fill_ to set -inf.
182	        self.allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
183	        self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
184	
185	        # req_index -> bad_words_token_ids
186	        self.bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]] = {}
187	
188	        self.req_output_token_ids: list[Optional[list[int]]] = []
189	
190	    @property
191	    def req_ids(self) -> list[str]:
192	        # None elements should only be present transiently
193	        # while performing state updates to the batch.
194	        return cast(list[str], self._req_ids)
195	
196	    def add_request(
197	        self,
198	        request: "CachedRequestState",
199	        req_index: Optional[int] = None,
200	    ) -> None:
201	        if req_index is None:
202	            req_index = self.num_reqs
203	        assert req_index < self.max_num_reqs
204	
205	        req_id = request.req_id
206	        if req_index == len(self._req_ids):
207	            self._req_ids.append(req_id)
208	            self.req_output_token_ids.append(request.output_token_ids)
209	        else:
210	            self._req_ids[req_index] = req_id
211	            self.req_output_token_ids[req_index] = request.output_token_ids
212	
213	        self.req_id_to_index[req_id] = req_index
214	
215	        # Copy the prompt token ids and output token ids.
216	        num_prompt_tokens = len(request.prompt_token_ids)
217	        self.num_prompt_tokens[req_index] = num_prompt_tokens
218	        self.token_ids_cpu[
219	            req_index, :num_prompt_tokens] = request.prompt_token_ids
220	        start_idx = num_prompt_tokens
221	        end_idx = start_idx + len(request.output_token_ids)
222	        self.token_ids_cpu[req_index,
223	                           start_idx:end_idx] = request.output_token_ids
224	        # Number of token ids in token_ids_cpu.
225	        # NOTE(woosuk): This may include spec decode tokens.
226	        self.num_tokens[req_index] = request.num_tokens
227	        # Number of tokens without spec decode tokens.
228	        self.num_tokens_no_spec[req_index] = request.num_tokens
229	
230	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[req_index] = request.num_computed_tokens
231	        self.block_table.add_row(request.block_ids, req_index)
232	
233	        sampling_params = request.sampling_params
234	        assert sampling_params is not None, "pooling requests not supported yet"
235	        if sampling_params.sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
236	            # Avoid later division by zero.
237	            self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = -1.0
238	            self.greedy_reqs.add(req_id)
239	        else:
240	            self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.temperature
241	            self.random_reqs.add(req_id)
242	
243	        self.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p
244	        if sampling_params.top_p < 1:
245	            self.top_p_reqs.add(req_id)
246	        top_k = sampling_params.top_k
247	        if 0 < top_k < self.vocab_size:
248	            self.top_k_reqs.add(req_id)
249	        else:
250	            top_k = self.vocab_size
251	        self.top_k_cpu[req_index] = top_k
252	        self.min_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.min_p
253	        self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
254	            req_index] = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
255	        if sampling_params.min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
256	            self.min_p_reqs.add(req_id)
257	        if sampling_params.frequency_penalty != 0.0:
258	            self.frequency_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
259	        self.presence_penalties_cpu[
260	            req_index] = sampling_params.presence_penalty
261	        if sampling_params.presence_penalty != 0.0:
262	            self.presence_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
263	        self.repetition_penalties_cpu[
264	            req_index] = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
265	        if sampling_params.repetition_penalty != 1.0:
266	            self.repetition_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
267	        if sampling_params.min_tokens:
268	            self.min_tokens[req_index] = (sampling_params.min_tokens,
269	                                          sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids)
270	
271	        # NOTE(woosuk): self.generators should not include the requests that
272	        # do not have their own generator.
273	        if request.generator is not None:
274	            self.generators[req_index] = request.generator
275	
276	        if sampling_params.logprobs is not None:
277	            self.num_logprobs[req_id] = sampling_params.logprobs
278	        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
279	            self.num_prompt_logprobs[req_id] = sampling_params.prompt_logprobs
280	        if sampling_params.logit_bias is not None:
281	            self.logit_bias[req_index] = sampling_params.logit_bias
282

417	    def condense(self, empty_req_indices: list[int]) -> None:
418	        """Move non-empty requests down into lower, empty indices.
419	        
420	        Args:
421	          empty_req_indices: empty batch indices, sorted descending.
422	        """
423	        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
424	        if num_reqs == 0:
425	            # The batched states are empty.
426	            self._req_ids.clear()
427	            self.req_output_token_ids.clear()
428	            return
429	
430	        # NOTE(woosuk): This function assumes that the empty_req_indices
431	        # is sorted in descending order.
432	        last_req_index = num_reqs + len(empty_req_indices) - 1
433	        while empty_req_indices:
434	            # Find the largest non-empty index.
435	            while last_req_index in empty_req_indices:
436	                last_req_index -= 1
437	
438	            # Find the smallest empty index.
439	            empty_index = empty_req_indices.pop()
440	            if empty_index >= last_req_index:
441	                break
442	
443	            # Swap the states.
444	            req_id = self._req_ids[last_req_index]
445	            output_token_ids = self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index]
446	            assert req_id is not None

449	            self.req_output_token_ids[empty_index] = output_token_ids
450	            self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index] = None

455	                last_req_index, :num_tokens]
456	            self.num_tokens[empty_index] = num_tokens
457	            self.num_tokens_no_spec[empty_index] = self.num_tokens_no_spec[
458	                last_req_index]
459	            self.num_prompt_tokens[empty_index] = self.num_prompt_tokens[
460	                last_req_index]

462	                empty_index] = self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[last_req_index]
463	            self.block_table.move_row(last_req_index, empty_index)

504	    def _make_prompt_token_ids_tensor(self) -> torch.Tensor:
505	        max_prompt_len = self.num_prompt_tokens[:self.num_reqs].max()
506	        prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
507	            (self.num_reqs, max_prompt_len),

511	        )
512	        prompt_token_ids = prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.numpy()
513	        prompt_token_ids[:] = self.token_ids_cpu[:self.
514	                                                 num_reqs, :max_prompt_len]
515	        # Use the value of vocab_size as a pad since we don't have a
516	        # token_id of this value.
517	        for i in range(self.num_reqs):
518	            prompt_token_ids[i, self.num_prompt_tokens[i]:] = self.vocab_size
519	        return prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.to(device=self.device,
520	                                              non_blocking=True)
